studyName: Prematurity, Respiratory Outcomes, Immune System, and Microbiome Study (PRISM)

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, visit, postmenstrual age at time of nsasal or rectal swab sampling, day of life of the nasal or rectal tests, body site, read count, analyte type, tumor  status, histological type, region of bacteria being sequenced, presence of bacteria or virus (Adenovirus, Bocavirus, Bordetella pertussis, Coronavirus 1-4, Chlamydophila pneumoniae, Enterobacteriaceae, Flu A, Flu B, Haemophilus influenzae, Human metapneumovirus, Mycoplasma hominis, Mycoplasma pneumoniae, parechovirus, PIV 1-3, Rhinovirus, RSV, Streptococcus pneumoniae, and Ureaplasma) within the sample, hard code to bacteria or virus needed for analysis, hospital admissions, due to wheezing or breathing problems, admitted to intensive care, admissions due to RSV, sick visits, sick visits due to wheezing or breathing problems or due to wheezing unknown, has baby chest sounded wheezy or whistling, occurred with colds, occurred apart from colds, chest wheezy during daytime or night time, diagnosed with wheezing by doctor, cough without cold, coughing during daytime or night time, number of colds, breathing and heart rate monitor, oxygen therapy, CPAP or BIPAP, ventilator, trach or breathing tube, feeding tube in nose or  stomach, other equipment used, is baby receiving breast milk, did baby receive breast milk since last visit, for how many months did baby receive breast milk for more than half of feedings, does baby eat any non-milk foods, and what non-milk foods does baby eat.

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, cohort, sex, ethnic, race, gestational age at birth, birth mode, Bronchopulmonary dysplasia status, persistent repiratory disease after one year, oxygen auc at 7 and 14 days, days on 7 variables (oxygen, mechanical ventilation, high frequency ventilation, cpap, nasal cannula gt 1 lmp, any respiratory support, and nasal cannula), maximum mean airway pressure, last PMA and last DOL on ETT for GT 2 days in a row, gestational age at discharge, exposure to furry animals in hte home, asthma, allergies, and eczema within the siblings, BMI of the mother at the time of delivery, chlorioamnionitis, season of birth, derived respiratory severity score, oxygen auc for first 14 days of life, and difference between FIO2 week 1 and FIO2 week 2.

variables.name: AUC14d
variables.description: OxygenAUC for first 14d of life

variables.name: AUCwk2_adj
variables.description: Difference between FIO2 week 2 and FIO2 week 1

variables.name: BirthMode
variables.description: Mode of Delivery

variables.name: Cohort
variables.description: Label indicating group of subject

variables.name: FinalStatus
variables.description: Bronchopulmonary dysplasia status

variables.name: PRD_1yr
variables.description: Persistent Repiratory disease after one year

variables.name: RMSS
variables.description: Derived respiratory severity score

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Unique Identifier

variables.description: Oxygen auc at 14 days

variables.description: Oxygen auc at 7 days

variables.description: Subject ethnicity

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
